definition: Tariffs simultaneously raise revenue ($120-280B after behavioral haircut), raise CPI (0.5-1.5%), and compress real growth — a regressive fiscal tightening plus inflationary supply shock. No standard macro regime captures this combination. The analytical framework is endorsed as underresearched but tariff policy uncertainty limits conviction.
evidence: $200-400B static revenue reduced to $120-280B after behavioral haircut (2018-19 precedent: 60-70% realization)
Pair_0 endorsed analytical framework as underresearched
Historical tariff revenue consistently 60-70% of static projections
open_questions: How do retaliatory tariffs alter the net fiscal and inflationary impact?
What is the general equilibrium effect through trade diversion channels?
